why: The canonical agent-class DID from docs/identity.md:14. A validator recovers the method and method-specific-id but CANNOT recover the class from the DID — that is the finding, not a bug in the vector.
A service-class DID carrying an INTERIOR hyphen (docs/identity.md:16). Proves Candidate A (forbid-hyphen) cannot be the collision fix: hyphenated ids already exist and share the flat namespace.
A hub-class DID using the informal dotted convention (docs/identity.md:15). '.' is legal in the deployed grammar and illegal in every base id alphabet — the property Candidate C would exploit.
A principal-class DID as minted by cortex peerDid (identity-registry.ts). The principal class has no IdentityType value, so a registry cannot type it — recorded as a finding.

This single string is simultaneously a valid stack DID (cortex.ts mint of stack andreas/meta-factory), a valid principal DID (peerDid('andreas-meta-factory')), and a valid agent DID. Byte-identical across classes — the proven collision guarded only at runtime (cortex identity-registry.ts add() is last-write-wins).
The synthetic system-class DID minted by cortex reflex-activation-listener.ts:68. A sixth de-facto class in the flat namespace with no registry type and no reservation.

DID_RE ACCEPTS a trailing hyphen (verified by execution). There is no length cap and no trailing-separator rule — a degenerate form this RFC records as an OPEN DECISION. Contrast principal-id, which forbids a trailing hyphen.
A stack whose slug is not `default` must round-trip. Guards cortex#1812.

MASKING CASE. A stack LITERALLY named `default` is legal and is NOT the fabricated sentinel. A stack named `default` masked cortex#1812 for two days because the fabricated value coincided with a real one for one party.
The live stack-DID mint (cortex.ts:1025-1027, `/`→`-`). Its output is byte-identical to render/principal-did-andreas-meta-factory below — the collision pair.

COLLISION PAIR with render/stack-did-andreas-meta-factory. A principal literally named `andreas-meta-factory` (legal under PRINCIPAL_RE) renders to the SAME DID as the stack andreas/meta-factory. The only defense is cortex's runtime trust-displacement guard.
A hyphenated principal (legal under PRINCIPAL_RE) with stack `dev`. Byte-identical to render/stack-did-first-hyphen-victim below — the hyphen-inside-principal collision.

COLLISION PAIR with render/stack-did-hyphenated-principal. cortex's first-hyphen decode (review-consumer.ts:1454) attributes did:mf:meta-factory-dev to principal `meta`, silently mis-routing the federated verdict — the decode claims 'the wire grammar forbids hyphenated principals', which PRINCIPAL_RE does not.
Byte-identical to encode/segment-dot-dash below — the subject-encoding collision that falsifies namespace.md:206/221's injectivity claim.

COLLISION PAIR with encode/segment-dash-dot. Two distinct DID_RE-valid DIDs encode to the same @-segment, so two assistants share one direct-routing subscription (local.{principal}.{stack}.tasks.@{assistant}.{capability}). Verified by execution.
